Operating Philosophy

Revenue growth.
Cost discipline.
Simultaneously.

I run multi-site retail and operations networks — commercial and operational, simultaneously. Full P&L accountability at USD 200M scale, across 12+ years in Zambia, India, and Saudi Arabia. Both sides of the business. One owner.

The results — USD 36M revenue uplift, 30% OPEX reduction, 32 months of record market share — came from making decisions early and holding the consequences. Not every call was clean. Rebuilding inventory governance while the network processed 20 million litres a month required deciding what to break first and what to protect.

My career spans petroleum retail at scale (Mount Meru Petroleum, Zambia), state-wide downstream oil network development (Reliance BP Mobility, India), and multi-site general management across QSR in the GCC. Three industries. Three countries. One consistent outcome.

These results are not market-specific. The same discipline — early decisions, full accountability, simultaneous commercial and operational delivery — has produced above-target outcomes across every environment I have operated in. The operating principles built across these networks apply directly across any multi-site, high-volume, margin-sensitive business. The conditions change. The outcome does not. Delivered consistently across multiple markets and operating environments — not dependent on a single context.

The standard I hold is simple: governance, accountability, and performance frameworks that function identically whether I am in the room or not — across 1 location or 90. I build operating systems that allow these outcomes to be repeated — not one-time results dependent on circumstance.

"The numbers report what happened. I stay close enough to know what they are hiding."

Retail Networks P&L Ownership Network Ops Revenue Growth OPEX Reduction Digital Transformation Sub-Saharan Africa GCC · UAE ERP · SAP · Odoo
Mount Meru Petroleum · Lusaka, Zambia

Target Roles & Markets

The Operating Model

  • Revenue growth and cost discipline — simultaneously
  • USD 36M growth + USD 3.6M+ savings in the same 24 months
  • 30% OPEX reduction while sustaining 15% annual growth
  • Systems built to function without my presence at every station
Scale & Impact at a Glance

Performance Indicators

P&L Owned End-to-End
$200M
Full OPEX/CAPEX accountability — 90 retail locations, 20 million units per month, board-level financial reporting. One of the largest independent multi-site retail networks in Sub-Saharan Africa.
Revenue Uplift Delivered
$36M
15% annual growth across 12+ consecutive quarters — unified commercial strategy, structured dealer incentives, and field performance reviews.
OPEX Reduction
30%
USD 3.6M+ saved annually — renegotiated 15+ vendor contracts, rebuilt SOPs across all 90 locations. Delivered in the same period as revenue acceleration, not sequentially.
Market Share — 32 Months
17.5%
Longest sustained market share record in company history — held through disciplined dealer performance management and competitor positioning at network level, not promotional pricing.
Stock Loss Eliminated
58%
Rebuilt inventory governance while the network continued processing 20 million units monthly. Zero throughput disruption — validated monthly against variance data.
Greenfield Locations Commissioned
16
All 16 delivered on schedule with zero post-handover compliance failures — full ownership from site evaluation and CAPEX planning through regulatory approvals and commissioning.
Operational Efficiency Gain
40%
Post-ERP deployment gain — Retail Automation, Auto-Indenting, and Audit Management directed across all 90 locations. Measured via pre/post KPI dashboards.
Fleet Card Programme Ramp
6 Mo
B2B Fleet Card Programme built from zero — framework, portal, dealer integration, network rollout. 30% network sales increase within 6 months. Fastest commercial ramp in company history.
Decisions That Moved the Business

Selected Impact

01
USD 36M revenue growth and 30% cost reduction — in the same 24-month window
Rejected the stabilise-first playbook. Rebuilt operations while accelerating commercial strategy. USD 36M revenue uplift + USD 3.6M+ OPEX savings — sustained for two consecutive years.
$36M Uplift
02
17.5% retail market share for 32 consecutive months — the longest in company history
Built through disciplined dealer performance management, structured incentive frameworks, and competitor positioning made at network level — not promotional pricing. A structural competitive advantage.
32 Months

Directed ERP, CRM, Retail Automation, and real-time KPI dashboards across 90 locations. 40% efficiency gain — shifted network management from reactive to data-driven.
40% Efficiency
04
Eliminated 58% of network-wide stock loss — rebuilt governance under full operating load
Rebuilt inventory governance while the network processed 20 million units monthly. Every change landed without disrupting throughput — validated against monthly variance data.
58% Loss Cut
05
Built the B2B Fleet Card Programme from zero — 30% network sales growth in 6 months
Owned full end-to-end go-to-market: framework design, portal build, dealer integration, and rollout. No inherited structure. Fastest commercial programme ramp in company history.
+30% Sales
06
15–20% annual sales growth across six consecutive years at Reliance BP
Directed a state-wide dealer and distribution network through volatility and regulatory change. Beat commercial targets by 10–15% consistently through structured incentive frameworks and multi-year strategy.
6 Yr Growth
07
Built an e-commerce delivery channel from zero — 500 million units annual volume within 24 months
Established a new digital revenue channel and end-to-end operational model from the ground up. Zero to 500 million units annually — a new business line built on conviction before commercial infrastructure existed to support it.
500M Litres
08
Directed supply chain continuity across the network — zero dry-outs under full operating load
Led supply chain and distribution management for six months across all retail locations while maintaining uninterrupted delivery to commercial (B2B) and enterprise customers simultaneously. Zero throughput failures across the network.
Zero Dry-Outs

Executive Judgment in Practice

Strategic Decisions

01
Stabilise or Accelerate — I Chose Both, Simultaneously
The conventional call was to stabilise first. I rejected it. Rebuilt operations and drove commercial growth simultaneously — USD 36M revenue uplift and 30% OPEX reduction in the same 24-month window.
02
Rebuilding Governance Under Full Operating Load
Eliminating 58% of stock loss required redesigning inventory governance while processing 20 million litres a month. Every change had to land without interrupting throughput — that constraint sharpens execution.
03
Launching a Revenue Channel Before the Data Justified It
The B2B Fleet Card Programme didn't exist. No framework, no customer base, no structure to inherit. I built it end-to-end before the data existed to justify it. Within 6 months: 30% network sales increase. Conviction separates a decision from a delay.
04
I Decide. I Own. I Deliver.
Every decision in this portfolio was made without escalation. I call it, I make it, I own it — regardless of outcome. Waiting for permission at this scale is not caution. It is failure.
05
Commissioning a 2 Million Litre Depot — Full Operational Readiness From Zero
Led end-to-end commissioning of a 2 million unit city distribution depot — team build-out, capability design, and full operationalisation through to throughput stabilisation. Delivered operational readiness without inheriting structure or precedent.
How Decisions Get Made

How I Operate

"Twelve years at this scale has produced one consistent finding: the quality of the decision in the hard moment is the only thing that determines everything else."

01
Execution clarity over reporting complexity
A clean decision made fast beats a perfect analysis made late. At 90 locations and USD 200M in revenue, analysis paralysis shows up in variance, throughput, and market share.
02
I own the outcome — before it happens, not after
I make decisions early and own the outcome completely. Distributing accountability after the fact is how organisations lose clarity on who is responsible.
03
Revenue and cost are the same conversation
Most operators sequence them. I run them simultaneously. The 30% OPEX reduction and USD 36M revenue growth happened in the same window. Separating the mandates creates the inefficiency you are trying to fix.
04
Systems must hold at scale
Scale reveals design. A system that works at one location must perform identically at 90. I build governance, accountability, and performance frameworks that don't require my presence to function.
05
Proximity is a management tool, not a preference
Data governs. Proximity reveals. I stay close enough to know when the numbers are lying — before reporting lag makes the problem expensive.
06
No escalation. Full accountability.
At USD 200M scale, deferred decisions cost market share and margin. I act early, absorb the consequences, and deliver. No committee required.
07
Data tools accelerate the decision — they do not make it
Where AI and data tools sharpen execution, I use them. Where they create noise or dependency, I don't. The judgment is mine. The tools serve it.
Functional Depth Across Three Disciplines

Core Capabilities

Twelve years of operating at this scale — across Sub-Saharan Africa, India, and the GCC — has produced functional depth in three disciplines that rarely sit in one role simultaneously.

Operations ownership at USD 200M. Commercial strategy across 90 stations. End-to-end digital transformation. Each delivered concurrently, not sequentially.

Progression of Scale & Responsibility

Career Journey

Career progression built through increasing scale, operational complexity, and full ownership of business performance.

Started with unit-level operations in QSR — building foundations in cost control, team capability, and execution discipline. Expanded into multi-site environments with responsibility for network performance, dealer management, and commercial delivery across distributed operations.

Progressed into large-scale multi-site retail and distribution networks — taking on full P&L accountability across multi-province operations and integrating commercial strategy, operational governance, and system-led execution.

Across 12 years the scope evolved from managing individual units to building and scaling enterprise-level operating systems across 90 locations, USD 200M revenue, and 1,200+ people.

Each step increased responsibility. The underlying focus remained constant — driving revenue growth and cost discipline simultaneously, under full operating load.

From unit operations to USD 200M P&L ownership — scaling systems, not just roles.

Retail & Multi-Site Operations Executive

Built for Scale.
Ready for the Next Mandate.

USD 200M P&L · 90 Stations · 12+ Years · 3 Countries

Operating at scale is not a future step — it is the current baseline.

I currently run a USD 200M multi-site business across 90 locations with full accountability across revenue, operations, and cost structure. The work is not functional — it is enterprise execution across commercial strategy, operational systems, and financial discipline.

The consistent outcome has been the ability to grow revenue while reducing cost without destabilising operations — delivered under full operating load, not in controlled environments.

This translates directly into mandates where businesses need

  • Scale without loss of control
  • Growth without cost expansion
  • Systems that hold beyond individual leadership

I am open to roles with full P&L accountability across multi-site or distributed operations — where the expectation is not to manage complexity, but to bring structure, discipline, and sustained performance to it.

If the mandate requires both growth and control at scale — we should have a conversation.

Built to own the mandate from day one — P&L accountability, operational turnaround, and scalable growth. The track record speaks before the conversation starts.
